_notes: Registry of models under test. Keys are the short names used by --models on the runner CLI.
All model IDs verified against provider docs 2026-07-17: platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview, developers.openai.com/api/docs/models, ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models.
The prereg's 'thinking budget' axis (low/med/high/extra) is realized per-model via thinking_mode:

- 'effort': Anthropic adaptive-thinking models (Opus 4.8/4.7, Sonnet 4.6/5). Passed as output_config.effort + thinking:{type:'adaptive'}. NOTE: manual budget_tokens returns HTTP 400 on Opus 4.8/4.7. Mapping: low->low, med->medium, high->high, extra->xhigh (max also exists, unused in prereg).
- null: no test-time reasoning control exposed (Gemini 2.5 Pro via basic generate_content here).

budget 'none' = no thinking config sent. On effort-mode models the API then runs without thinking (documented behavior); effort is left unset (API default 'high' applies to token spend only). Recorded in manifest.
Per prereg 4.4: API only, no system prompt (system=null), provider-default temperature (record it), n=5 samples.

fable-5.note: Newest/most capable Claude (Mythos-class tier above Opus). Adaptive thinking is ALWAYS ON and cannot be disabled (thinking:{type:'disabled'} rejected) — the 'none' budget cell still thinks at default effort; there is no true nonthinking condition for this model. Record this asymmetry in analysis.

sonnet-4.6.note: Legacy per docs (current Sonnet is claude-sonnet-5) but still served; prereg names Sonnet 4.6.

sonnet-5.note: Current Sonnet; added to the full-study battery (prereg v0.2.4) for the within-family capability ladder.

openai-flagship.note: Current OpenAI flagship (alias gpt-5.6). reasoning_effort levels include low/medium/high/xhigh/max.

gemini-2.5-pro.note: Stable Pro ('most advanced for complex tasks') per ai.google.dev 2026-07-17. Thinking controls not wired for Gemini in this runner; runs without explicit thinking config.

gemini-3.5-flash.note: Google's newest/most intelligent stable model per ai.google.dev 2026-07-17.

gemini-3.1-flash-lite.note: Stable 3.1-generation Flash variant. NOTE: user asked for 'gemini 3.1 flash' — no stable gemini-3.1-flash exists; this Lite variant (or gemini-3.1-pro-preview) is the closest. Confirm choice before full study.
